response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned software developer with roughly 15 years of experience, but their background is overwhelmingly in web and full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, Ember.js). Direct C++ experience is limited to a short 7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 and a bachelor thesis project, far short of the 8-12 years of hands-on C++/STL depth this role requires. There is no evidence of MQTT, message-broker architectures at industrial scale, Secure Development Lifecycle practice, or IEC 62443 familiarity. While the low-latency DLang work at Dunnhumby shows strong systems instincts, the profile does not match a senior C++ role in industrial automation with security responsibilities.
strengths: Strong systems-level experience with low-latency, high-throughput services (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Dunnhumby
Broad engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source maintainership
Long, varied career spanning embedded, desktop, web, and mobile with proven ownership (founder of GISCollective)
concerns: Minimal recent C++ experience — last professional C++ work was in 2015, and STL/modern C++ depth is unproven
No industrial automation, IoT protocol (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus), or IEC 62443 / SDL security background
Career trajectory centers on web/full-stack and AI tooling, suggesting a poor domain fit and likely mismatch of interests

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity or IEC 62443 exposure
